title: Solid state pelletizing for Bi-doped strontium molybdate synthesis and photocatalytic application
author: Acosta-Vergara, Torres-Palma, et al.

steps.title: Methodology for photocatalytic application with Rhodamine B
steps.instruction: The photocatalytic activity of Sr1‚àí3x Bi2x–§xMoO4 (0¬Ý‚â§¬Ýx¬Ý‚â§¬Ý0,025) system by the degradation of Rhodamine B in water solution (350¬ÝW lamp HPL-N (Philips), was evaluated.a.The lamp emits to 365¬Ýnm with an energy of 4¬ÝeV.b.An amount of 0.2¬Ýg of substrate to 200¬ÝmL for RhB solution (10¬Ýmg/L) in a double layer glass tumbler, was added.c.Before illumination with the lamp, the suspensions were magnetically stirred for ~ 30¬Ýmin by a magnetic stirrer (~ 400¬Ýrpm, to establishment an adsorption-desorption equilibrium between the photocatalyst and the RhB solution.d.The total irradiation time was of 30¬Ýmin, and tested in 1, 2, 5, 10, 15, 20 and 30¬Ýmin. The samples were filtered and closed in test tubes to be analyzed using the Unico 2800 spectrophometer at 554¬Ýnm wavelength.e.The absorbances and statistically analyzed to calculate the percentage of decolorization (D) of RhB, were recorded.f.The temperature during the photocatalysis was 40,0¬Ý¬±¬Ý1 ¬∫C, controlled by a water recirculation system with a cooling system.g.Excess oxygen was supplied during the suspension reaction using a corundum air diffuser aeration system.

description:
The photocatalytic activity of the Sr1‚àí3xBi2xŒ¶xMoO4 system was investigated for oxidation of Rhodamine B. showed for all compositions a pseudo-first-order kinetic model, with the concentration of RhB changing according to the rate law, Eq.¬Ý(2).(2)C=C0e‚àíktwhere C is the concentration of RhB at time t, C0 is the initial concentration of RhB and k is the reaction rate. The sample with x=0.15 presented a best adjust when oxidizing RhB in an aqueous medium at pH 3.0. In the Fig.¬Ý8a is showed that for a stoichiometric X¬Ý=¬Ý0.15 and 0.20, the degradation is higher with relationship to Photolysis. However, for stoichiometric X¬Ý=¬Ý0.05, 0.10 and 0.25 the degradation does not enhance. This behavior is correlated with minor value Eg and minor particle size. In studies on photocatalysis processes, comparison of the results of the photolysis, adsorption and photocatalytic mechanisms is important. Thus, the mentioned processes were respectively differentiated in the experiments in the presence of UV radiation, but without a catalyst to assay the photolysis process and in dark condition to evaluate the adsorption process. Based on the 4.1%, 20.4%, and 92.7% removal efficiency for adsorption, photolysis, and photocatalysis, respectively, RhB removal by Sr1‚àí3xBi2x‚àÖxMoO4
X¬Ý=¬Ý0.15 occurs mainly through photocatalytic degradation. The effects of initial Sr1‚àí3xBi2x‚àÖxMoO4 (x¬Ý=¬Ý0.05- 0.25), pH (3, 5, 7, 9), dosses catalyst (20, 50, 100, 200, 300¬Ýmg), and UV-C irradiation time (0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25 and 30¬Ýmin) were optimized in a batch photoreactor, using RhB with 10¬Ýmg/L, figure b, c, d and e. The photoreactor was a rectangular cubic shape, made from Plexiglas, and had internal dimensions of 50¬Ýcm (length), 25¬Ýcm (width), and 2.5¬Ýcm (height), an applicable volume of 300¬ÝmL, and three UV lamps (low pressure, 6¬ÝW, Philips) that were placed on top of the reactor.
